text.title: cost-optimization Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for cost-optimization as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Semantic cache false positives: "what is the capital of France?" and "what is the capital of Germany?" may have cosine similarity greater than 0.95 and return the wrong cached answer.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Semantic caching threshold tuning requires offline evaluation on your actual query distribution. A threshold of 0.95 is often too aggressive — test with 0.98+ for factual queries.

failure-mode.description: Routing classifier overhead exceeds savings on short tasks.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If the classifier takes 200 tokens to route a 50-token task, the overhead can exceed the savings from cheaper model selection. Only route when task token count significantly exceeds classification overhead.

failure-mode.description: Prompt caching invalidated by a single space change in the system prompt.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: OpenAI and Claude require exact byte-for-byte prefix matches. Dynamic content injected into the static system prompt prefix (e.g., current date, user name) invalidates the cache on every call.

failure-mode.description: Human-in-loop checkpoint: when daily budget hits 80%, alerting after the automatic shutdown has already corrupted pipeline state.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Alert at 80% to allow human intervention before shutdown. Shutdown mid-pipeline corrupts in-flight state. Design graceful shutdown: complete current steps, then pause new intake.

failure-mode.description: Response caching with temperature greater than 0 returns stale creative outputs that users notice.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Temperature greater than 0 caching returns stale creative outputs users notice. Cache only at temperature=0.

failure-mode.description: Aggressive token reduction (removing "please", verbose instructions) can break models trained on verbose instruction-following.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Token reduction saves 20-40%, but removing instructions can degrade output quality. Test that removal doesn't degrade output quality.
